lines: Search now powered by the kernel: transcript search runs a hybrid BM25 + semantic pass directly in the kernel — no external vector store needed.
Per-show transcription toggle now lives in the kernel — overrides survive library rebuilds.
Podcast categories are now stored in the kernel for durability and sync.
Now Playing artwork no longer crashes playback metadata when Control Center or the lock screen asks for it.
OPML imports now skip bad feed rows without losing the rest of your list, and exports avoid duplicate feed URLs.
Feed URL subscriptions now reject unsupported links immediately and catch duplicates even when you omit https://.
Feeds that declare non-UTF-8 text encodings now refresh and subscribe with the right titles instead of dropping characters.
Failed download deletes now keep the episode marked as downloaded and show the failure in Diagnostics.
Relay editing now lives in one kernel-backed App Relays screen instead of a duplicate Agent Podcasts list.
Download rows now appear immediately after you tap Download.
Home Inbox now shows when the agent last finished triaging your episodes.
Saved shows and downloaded badges now stay visible while the app finishes launching.
Remote signer setup now keeps showing connection progress until the signer finishes pairing or the attempt times out.
Conversation and feedback participants now show real names and avatars instead of raw hex keys.
Your podcast list no longer flashes to an empty state while the app finishes launching.
Downloaded episodes now stay marked as downloaded after restarting the app.
Agent access control now persists reliably — approved and blocked peers survive app restarts and are enforced at the kernel level
Profile photo and podcast artwork uploads are now faster and more reliable — they go through the secure kernel upload pipeline instead of Swift HTTP, with NIP-46 bunker support.
The Social tab now populates automatically on sign-in — no more tapping Refresh to see who you follow. Notes from people you follow are now marked as trusted.
Chapter boundaries, summaries, and ad-span detection are now all generated by the kernel — faster, more accurate, and unified in a single LLM round-trip.
Playback position, library updates, and settings changes now reach the UI in real-time via per-domain push frames — a playback tick is ~1KB instead of multi-MB.
The Inbox section now shows a loading shimmer while the AI triage pass is running — no more silent wait with no feedback.
Fixed identity signing (avatar changes, NIP-46 bunker operations) silently failing since the v0.4.0 kernel upgrade — sign-for-return results are now delivered correctly.
Fixed the Now Playing home-screen widget showing an idle state while an episode was actually playing — it now updates live with the current episode, progress, and play state.
Sidebar Podcasts now navigates to the full list instead of opening a separate sheet — tap back to return home.
Add Show sheet fills the screen properly and the segment picker sits flush with the content — no more empty gap below search results.
The Now Playing home-screen widget now stays accurate straight from the player, and shows an "N to listen" badge when nothing's playing.
The Models settings list is cleaner: each role now shows just its selected model and provider, without extra capability chips.
Episode rows now show when downloads are waiting, moving, or failed, and when transcripts are waiting, running, ready, or failed. Download progress jumps now animate instead of snapping.
Your identity is now created automatically on first launch — no more "No Identity" limbo. Tapping "Generate Key Pair" works again too.
Auto skip ads is now on by default — if you’ve been listening without it, your next play will automatically skip detected ad breaks.

Episode Diagnostics is now a full picture of an episode: a new "Pipeline configuration" panel spells out exactly what will (or won’t) happen and which services it’ll use — and plainly tells you when nothing is set up to run.

Every pipeline event now names the service behind it — which transcription provider ran, which model generated the chapters — plus new playback, search-indexing, and clip events so anything that happens to an episode leaves a clear trace.
On-device (Apple) transcription now actually finishes — previously it could start and then hang forever without ever producing a transcript.

Subscribing to a podcast is now instant — the show appears in your library the moment you tap, and its episodes fill in a moment later in the background instead of making you wait for the feed to download.
Episode Diagnostics now explains when transcription was deliberately skipped — category turned off, AI transcription disabled, a missing provider key, or audio not downloaded yet.
Download now shows a progress bar immediately when queued — no more silent waiting
Swiping on a queued download now shows Cancel instead of a second Download button
OS-interrupted downloads (network drop, backgrounding) now properly surface as paused instead of silently vanishing
AssemblyAI and Perplexity settings now show whether keys were connected manually or through BYOK.
Agent scheduled tasks now persist across app restarts, including disabled, edited, and deleted task rows.
Agent scheduled tasks now use the shared backend scheduler, so prompt tasks and run timing stay consistent across platforms.
Transcript fallback messages now reflect the backend's resolved speech provider, so missing-key fallbacks are clearer.
Ollama endpoint settings now normalize through the shared backend, so cloud and local Ollama URLs behave consistently across devices.
On-device model choices now come from the shared backend, keeping local AI downloads and model selection consistent across platforms.
Speech model choices now come from the shared backend, keeping transcription and narration settings consistent across platforms.
Provider setup indicators now use the shared backend's loaded-key status, keeping Settings, transcripts, and wiki readiness in sync.
ElevenLabs voice previews, pick narration, and generated podcast speech now use the shared backend for TTS setup and provider errors.
ElevenLabs voice discovery now uses the shared backend, so voice lists load consistently across iOS and Android.
Provider model picks now save the backend-routed model ID, so OpenRouter and Ollama selections run through the intended shared provider.
Agent web search now uses the shared backend for Perplexity and OpenRouter fallback, with provider setup errors handled consistently.
AssemblyAI transcription now uses the shared backend for credential lookup, model routing, and error handling — consistent with all other STT providers.
ElevenLabs Scribe transcription now uses the shared backend, including your selected Scribe model and consistent provider errors.
Nostr profile and account actions now use the same backend-owned account key across app surfaces.
Nostr identity now restores with the correct account identifier, keeping profile lookup, signing, and sign-out on the same backend-owned key.
ElevenLabs key validation in Settings now uses the shared backend, matching provider setup errors across iPhone, Android, and the terminal app.
Episode Diagnostics can now retry OpenRouter Whisper transcription and show the backend's setup error instead of hiding the option when no key is configured.
OpenRouter key validation in Settings now uses the shared backend for missing-key and validation errors.
AI provider errors for wiki pages, chapters, categories, and clips now come from the shared backend, so OpenRouter and Ollama setup feedback is more consistent.
Auto-download now actually works — shows you’ve enabled it for pull their latest episodes on launch and the moment you turn the toggle on, instead of waiting for a manual refresh.
Downloaded episodes now transcribe themselves automatically, which kicks off chapter and ad-break detection — no need to open each episode first.
Episode Diagnostics now shows the full processing timeline — every download, transcription, and identification step is logged so you can see exactly what happened.
The agent can now skip forward/back, seek to any position, and tell you what’s currently playing
External RSS feeds the agent looks up now stay accurate without quietly adding those shows to your subscriptions.
Clippings now shows every clip — clips of episodes that have since dropped out of a feed no longer vanish behind an empty date heading.

Big libraries are dramatically faster — updating the library after playing, starring, or a background refresh is now ~30× quicker on a multi-thousand-episode library (it no longer slows down as your library grows).
Snappier all around — marking played, starring, and subscribing no longer briefly freeze the app while your library is large; the heavy work now happens off the main thread.
Fixed on-device AI models failing on iPhone — a downloaded local model now loads and answers instead of erroring out.
On-device models now actually run: pick a downloaded local model for a role (e.g. Agent) and that role does its thinking on your device instead of the cloud.
On-device models are reorganized: download them under Settings → Providers → Local, then pick one per role (Agent, Memory, Wiki, and more) in Settings → Models, right alongside your cloud providers.
Downloading episodes is now much smoother — the app no longer pegs the CPU (or heats up) while a download is in progress
On-device models (Gemma) now finish downloading and stay downloaded — fixed a bug where they got stuck at 100% and showed as not downloaded again after a restart.
Your Nostr key is now held by the app's secure kernel and never leaves it — stronger key safety across sign-in, profile, and feedback.
CarPlay now shows the chapter list as soon as chapters are ready, even when they arrive after the episode starts
Pressing Play from the lock screen right after reopening the app now correctly saves your position and marks episodes played
Your Nostr profile name, bio, and picture now sync across your devices
Run AI locally on your device with downloadable Gemma 4 models — private, offline, no API key.
Download progress now updates live instead of staying stuck at 0%
New Settings > Debug screen with an in-app log viewer — flip on debug logging to capture recent activity, then search, copy, or clear it
Your podcasts are now one tap away in the sidebar, with a See All view for the full list
Smoother library scrolling during playback — snapshot diffing now runs off the main thread
Your profile, notes, and clips now publish to Nostr through the app core for faster, more reliable signing
Your older agent-generated episodes now stick around after updating the app
Your relay edits now stick — added, removed, and re-roled relays survive an app restart
Editing and deleting agent-owned podcasts now updates and publishes correctly
Ask the agent to summarize an episode — summaries are generated and saved for next time
Agent-generated podcasts now publish as NIP-F4 — the canonical Nostr podcast standard
Configure app relays in Settings → Networking → App Relays
Your agent's Nostr relay is now a clearly labeled "Agent Relay" row in Settings → Agent — tap it to view or change the relay, with a note on what it's used for.
Nostr show discovery is now fully reactive — results appear as relays respond.
Home now has a "Recommended for you" row — AI-scored episode picks from across your shows, ordered by how well they match what you've been listening to.
Episode pages now show AI-generated category tags so you can tell at a glance what an episode is about.

Podcasts you publish to Nostr now keep their identity across restarts — each owned show's signing key is saved to your device, so re-launching the app no longer regenerates a fresh key or orphans your previously published shows.

Transcription now works out of the box with no API key — new installs default to Apple's on-device speech engine for downloaded episodes. If you've chosen a cloud transcription provider but haven't added its key yet, we now fall back to on-device transcription instead of doing nothing.

AI chapters now read the episode transcript to name real topic sections, instead of slicing the episode into equal blank chunks. If the AI can't be reached, you'll still get evenly-spaced chapters as a fallback.

Your Inbox now sorts itself with AI automatically — episodes are prioritized in the background as soon as they arrive, with no need to tap Triage, and it quietly falls back to newest-first when the AI is unavailable.
Episode category tags are now picked by AI — titles and descriptions are read and labeled with topics like Technology or True Crime, instead of simple keyword matching.
Voice mode now talks back — ask a question out loud and the assistant answers with a short spoken reply, using your podcast library.
For You picks are now chosen by AI — episodes are scored for interest with a one-line reason, instead of just showing the newest.
Your comments and posts now actually reach the Nostr relays — publishing and fetching over Nostr works directly from your iPhone.
Your default playback speed and auto-delete-after-played preference now sync across devices via iCloud.
Auto-download now honours your per-show Wi-Fi-only setting — episodes won't start downloading on cellular unless you've allowed it.
If on-device storage can't be opened, the app now warns you that this session won't be saved instead of failing silently.
Playback engine refactored: the Rust kernel now drives position tracking, ad-skip decisions, and auto-advance for more reliable playback state across sessions.
Now Playing widget now updates immediately when a show's artwork or title loads — no more showing a blank image until the next chapter change.
Detected ad segments now persist across app restarts — auto-skip works immediately on replay without re-analyzing the transcript.
Chapter fetching now works for all subscribed episodes, not just those loaded from RSS — fixes missing chapters on recently added shows.
Per-podcast auto-download toggle now persists correctly across restarts.
Chapter markers and transcripts now survive feed refreshes — no more re-loading after a refresh.
Social tab now loads your real Nostr follow list — contacts and their profile photos pulled live from the relay.
Your inbox now uses AI to rank episodes — each item gets a priority score and categories based on the episode content.
Play Latest is temporarily hidden from Siri and Shortcuts until its Rust-backed action is wired.
Shortcut cleanup keeps unsupported playback actions out until the kernel owns the policy.
Siri and Shortcuts can now pause, resume, and skip podcast playback.
Downloads now report live queued, paused, active, and failed states from the Rust kernel.
Show pages now explain why Follow failed instead of silently dismissing the error.
Home Inbox now shows when the agent last triaged episodes, how many picks it kept, and how many it archived.
What's New sheet now surfaces recent changes so you always know what landed since your last launch.
OpenRouter API key now saves correctly to Keychain during onboarding — AI features activate immediately after setup.
Fixed: restored mini-player episodes wait until you press play before starting the background download.
Local Ollama endpoints now work without an API key, and model discovery uses your configured Ollama host.
Lock screen and Control Center now show the episode and podcast name while playing
Podcast descriptions now display on the show page — rich show notes with HTML stripped clean
All Episodes now supports search — filter by episode or podcast name
Bookmark any episode — tap the bookmark icon or swipe to save it. Find all bookmarks from the Library toolbar.
Skip intervals are now user-configurable — set your preferred skip-forward and skip-back durations from Settings
Siri Skip Forward now works even when the app is backgrounded — position arithmetic moved to the Rust kernel.
Show notes no longer display raw HTML tags — the kernel now strips tags and decodes entities before delivering episode descriptions to the app.

Episodes without published chapters now offer a Generate Chapters button — once a transcript is fetched, the kernel synthesizes evenly-spaced AI chapters with a sparkles badge so you can tell them apart from publisher chapters.
Live Activity: see what's playing on your lock screen and Dynamic Island.
Social: see who you're following on Nostr.
CarPlay support — your library and per-show episodes now appear on the head unit, with one-tap play that hands off to the standard Now Playing controls.
Edit your Nostr profile and publish it to the network.
Handoff — start an episode on iPhone and pick up where you left off on iPad or Mac.
Configure your NIP-65 relay list from Identity → Advanced → Relays — add or remove the relays where your Nostr posts get published.
Fixed: the 2.5× and 3.0× playback-speed chips now actually apply — they were silently capped at 2.0× by the player kernel.
Episode transcripts: tap the speech-bubble in the episode detail toolbar to read a time-synced transcript, with the current segment highlighted and tap-to-seek on every row.
Chat with the agent from the new Agent tab — ask a question and the agent replies in a chat thread (LLM coming soon).
Downloads: manage all your saved episodes in one place.
Episodes now remember where you left off — pause or close the app mid-listen and the play button switches to Resume with the saved time shown in the episode list and detail.
We're now Pod0 — the app's display name has changed from Podcastr; your library, account, and downloads carry over untouched.
Get notified when new episodes arrive.
Episode detail now shows show notes — the description from the RSS feed appears below the play button so you can read what the episode is about before pressing play.
Pull-to-refresh keeps your podcast feeds up to date.
Experimental: the Add Show sheet can now search Nostr NIP-F4 podcast events and subscribe via their RSS feed tags.
AI wiki: open any show and tap the new book icon to start building a topic-by-topic knowledge base — full LLM synthesis is a follow-up, but you can generate, browse, and delete articles today.
Inbox tab: a new "what to listen to next" view ranks fresh episodes across every show you follow — swipe to dismiss, swipe right to mark listened.
Full-screen playback now shows episode chapter markers when a show publishes them, with one-tap seeking between chapters.
Transcripts: tap the speech-bubble button on the mini-player to open the episode transcript — fetched and parsed entirely by the Rust kernel.
Downloaded episodes now show as downloaded in the library — completed downloads report their on-disk path back to the kernel.
Up Next: build a playback queue from any show — swipe right on an episode (or long-press) to add it, then tap the new queue button on the mini-player to play next or remove items.
Episode comments: read and post Nostr comments on any episode.
Per-show auto-download — open any show, tap the ••• menu, and turn on Auto-download to have new episodes queued for offline listening automatically on the next feed refresh.
New Home tab with AI agent picks — newest episodes across your shows surfaced in a horizontal rail, plus a Continue Listening row and a fresh New Episodes list.
Search across your podcast library — a new magnifying-glass button in Library lets you find episodes by topic, host, or any text in the show notes.
AI Episodes — generate short narrated episodes about any topic from Identity → Advanced → Agent. Tap to listen via on-device speech.
Capture and share clips from any episode — open the player and tap the scissors chip to set start/end manually or hit Auto Snip to grab the current ±30 seconds.
Onboarding subscribe step now dispatches through the Rust kernel — first-run subscriptions feed the live library snapshot just like in-app subscribes
Library gained an All Episodes view — every episode across every subscribed show, newest first — plus a dedicated episode detail screen with artwork, metadata, and one-tap play.
Agent identity (name, bio, profile picture, relay URL) now persists across app launches — previously it was held only in memory and reset on relaunch.
Library is now fully powered by the Rust kernel — legacy compat stores removed, episode list backed entirely by real-time NMP snapshots
Episodes can now be downloaded for offline listening
Import podcasts from an OPML file and export your library
Your podcast library now survives app restarts — subscriptions, episodes, and refresh state persist across launches.
Tap the mini-player to open a full-screen now-playing view with a scrubber, skip-back-15 / skip-forward-30 transport, playback speed, sleep timer, and AirPlay routing.
Library grid and show detail now pull live from the Rust kernel — subscribe, refresh, and iTunes search all work without a local database
Play, pause, seek, and skip controls are now powered by the NMP Rust kernel — playback state updates reliably across the mini-player and anywhere else the player appears.
Mini-player: tap play/pause or skip forward 30 seconds directly from the bar above the tab bar.
Sidebar no longer dims the rest of the app when open, and the mini-player has been streamlined — the thin progress strip at its bottom edge is gone.
Fixed: agent-generated downloaded episodes now play reliably across app launches — stale sandbox paths are recomputed at playback time.
Fixed: agent-generated episodes now correctly upload audio to Blossom before publishing to Nostr — local file paths no longer appear in kind:30075 events.
Discover and subscribe to podcasts published on Nostr (NIP-74) from the Add Show sheet — tap the new Nostr tab.
NIP-74 podcast events now publish to your Nostr outbox relays (NIP-65). Manage the relay list in Settings → Agent → Podcasts.
Episode and podcast titles no longer appear in ALL CAPS — natural casing throughout.
Artwork placeholders are now consistent across the app: neutral waveform icon on a soft gray background.

Fixed: generate_podcast_artwork now works with Gemini (Nano Banana) models — they use the chat API, not the image endpoint. The model picker in Settings → Image Generation now shows only image-capable models.
Avatar in the top-left toolbar now shows cleanly without the oversized glass ring.
Settings gear icon is now in the toolbar next to your avatar, one tap away from anywhere in the app.
Polished episode artwork placeholder: now uses a neutral waveform icon instead of a random orange/purple gradient.
Mini player is now larger with bigger fonts. Tap × to dismiss it and remove the episode from Continue Listening.
Agent tool: publish_episode publishes an existing episode to Nostr as a NIP-74 kind:30075 event.
Agent tool: update_podcast now retroactively publishes all existing episodes when a podcast's visibility is flipped to public.
Agent tools create_podcast and update_podcast now return a shareable naddr (NIP-19) for the published show event.
Mini player is now slightly larger and more comfortable to tap.
Removed the seek sound effect.
Fixed: opening the app no longer interrupts audio playing in other apps.
Remote signer setup now includes a QR code flow — open your signer app (Amber, Primal, nsec.app) and scan instead of copy-pasting a bunker URI.
The agent can now create and manage its own podcasts — complete with AI-generated cover art and optional Nostr publishing (NIP-74). Use Settings > Agent > Podcasts to control per-show visibility.
Tap ••• on any show page and choose 'Download all episodes' to queue every episode for offline listening and transcription in one tap.
New Library in the sidebar — browse every episode across all your podcasts in one scrollable list, with filters for Unplayed, In Progress, Downloaded, and Starred.
New Bookmarks screen in the sidebar — every episode you've starred, clipped, or annotated appears in one place, with chips showing what's there.
Fixed: the Account ID QR code now appears correctly when you tap the QR button on the Identity screen.
The mini-player now remembers which episode you were listening to and restores it automatically after an app restart — just tap play to resume.
Player: tap the + note button in the chapter rail to jot a note anchored to your exact playhead position. Agent-saved notes appear inline too — chronologically interleaved with the chapters.
Player: tap the episode title to open episode details — no more crash.
Player: swipe right on chapters to see show notes, swipe left to go back — no more tabs.
Your updated profile photo now appears immediately after saving.
Nostr conversation transcripts now show all messages left-aligned with avatar and sender name, like Slack.
New sidebar: tap your avatar to navigate to Clippings, Wiki, and Settings. Search moved to the top-right toolbar.
Settings › Agent › Tasks: view, create, edit, and delete the agent's scheduled tasks.
Swipe right in the player to read show notes; swipe left to return to chapters.
Agent conversations now open at the most recent message, with a scroll-to-bottom button when you've scrolled up.
The agent can now download YouTube videos as podcast episodes via a self-hosted extractor — activate the new youtube_ingestion skill and configure your endpoint in Settings.

CarPlay support is here. Listen Now picks up where you left off, Shows lists your subscriptions alphabetically, and Downloads keeps the offline-ready episodes one tap away when cell coverage drops. The system Now Playing screen gets two extra buttons: a speed cycle (1×/1.3×/1.5×/1.7×/2×) and a chapter list that seeks the playhead on tap. CarPlay activation on real hardware still requires Apple to approve the carplay-audio entitlement against the app — until then, plug into the simulator's CarPlay display to try it.
When your agent messages another agent, it now automatically resumes the conversation once the other agent replies — no manual follow-up needed.
The agent now shows each friend's 6-character pubkey prefix in the Friends list and can message them using just that prefix.
When the agent activates a skill (like Wiki Research or Podcast Generation) during a chat, you'll now see a small indicator in the conversation so you know which capabilities are in play.
The agent now has a 'conversation_history' skill — ask it to recall what you discussed in a past session and it can list and search across all your in-app and Nostr conversation threads.

play_external_episode is gone — the agent now uses a single play_episode tool for everything. Pass episode_id for library episodes, audio_url + title for one-off URLs; start_seconds / end_seconds / queue_position work the same in both cases.
Tap 'See All' on the Inbox section header to open a dedicated inbox view showing every episode the agent queued for you, with its rationale.
The agent can now send a Nostr message to a friend from any context — just ask it to message someone and it will. Previously it would refuse unless you were already inside a peer Nostr conversation.

Swipe left on any Continue Listening row to remove it from the list without marking it played. The episode stays in your library; your progress is cleared. The section is now 'Subscriptions' → 'Podcasts' with slightly larger artwork.

When your agent is talking to a friend over Nostr, a 'Talking to …' capsule now flashes at the top of the screen and a Conversations button appears in the top toolbar so you can jump into the live thread.

Similarity search and 'more like this' now find untranscribed episodes too — every episode's title + description is embedded into the search index, so back-catalog episodes from a fresh subscription surface alongside transcribed ones.
AI Inbox: archived episodes now stay archived across feed refreshes, and silently-archived episodes no longer ping you with a notification or kick off an auto-download.
Fixed: the agent now correctly responds to all Nostr messages in a conversation, not just the first one.

New AI Inbox on Home: the agent autonomously triages new episodes from your subscriptions — surfacing the ones worth your time with a one-line rationale, silently archiving the rest. No review queue, full autonomy.
The agent can now schedule recurring tasks — tell it to check Hacker News daily, curate your queue weekly, or anything else on a cadence.
The + button to add a new podcast has moved to the All Podcasts list.
Opening a podcast you don't follow now automatically loads all its published episodes.
Tap the episode title in the player to jump straight to the episode's detail page.
Agent playback now uses a single play tool with start, end, and queue-position (now / next / end) — ask the agent to 'play the intro then add the interview to up next' in one breath.
The agent can now list every podcast in the app (not just the ones you subscribe to) and can delete any of them on request.
Asking the agent to transcribe an off-library episode no longer silently subscribes you to the show.
Episode titles in the player are no longer displayed in all-caps.

Opening a show you don't follow now adapts to that state: the toolbar offers a Follow action instead of subscription toggles, and the destructive option reads Delete (removes the podcast plus its episodes) rather than Unsubscribe. Settings stays hidden until you actually follow the show — there's no subscription row to mutate yet.

Fixed: when you ask the agent about a podcast you don't follow, it can now list its episodes without secretly subscribing you to it. Previously the only way for the agent to look up episodes was to subscribe to the show first, which left unwanted follows behind.

Hit play on any episode and the app now downloads the audio in the background while you stream — transcripts and chapters follow automatically, no more waiting for a manual download tap before they show up.

Fixed: the player's More menu Go to episode and Go to show items no longer crash. The two items used to round-trip through a deep-link while the player was still mid-dismiss; they now hand the request to the root view directly so the player sheet hands off cleanly to the detail surface.

Home's Subscriptions section now has a See All entry that opens an All Podcasts list — every podcast the app knows about, including shows from one-off external plays you never subscribed to. Swipe any row to delete the podcast plus all its episodes; a Following chip marks the ones you actively follow.

Feedback grew up: tap the new mic button to dictate a feedback thread, switch the Mine/Everyone segment and other people's threads now show their display name, and inside a thread you'll see avatars, name headers, and properly rendered markdown — with image URLs in replies showing inline instead of as raw text.

Profile photos are no longer locked to generated styles — Edit Profile → Change photo → Choose from library now actually uploads. Your pick is resized to 800px JPEG, signed, and uploaded to a Blossom host, and the returned URL goes straight into your kind-0 profile.

Podcast identity is now decoupled from your follow list. Episodes the agent added without subscribing to the show — including external plays and AI-generated shows — now carry the source podcast's real artwork and title everywhere on Home, Library, and Player. The library is about episodes; subscribing is a separate user choice.

Home now shows a Continue Listening strip at the top — up to 3 in-progress episodes from the last 2 weeks, with a See All screen where you can swipe to dismiss any you're done with. The Featured section now shows only agent picks, and the podcast list is slightly larger.
Agent-generated episodes now show a source chip in the player — tap it to jump to the in-app conversation or Nostr thread that commissioned the podcast.

Your Nostr agent now uses the exact same toolset as your in-app chat — peers you've approved can ask it to generate a podcast for you, search your library, compile a wiki page, save a note, or anything else the agent can do, and it acts on your behalf. Its prompt now starts with a peer-context preamble (who it's talking to, that they're NOT you) before the usual owner-voice inventory, so it addresses the peer by their kind:0 display name while still doing the work for you.

Your Nostr agent now actually replies. When an approved pubkey messages your agent, it reads the thread, asks your configured LLM for a response, signs and publishes a NIP-10 kind:1 reply, and stamps both turns into the Conversations transcript — with a hard 10-outgoing-turn cap per thread and built-in dedup so a flaky relay can't trigger duplicate replies.
Friend detail now shows your full Nostr conversation history with that friend.
Agent consolidates accumulated memories into a tight summary it carries forward.
Agent run history is now saved across launches, with filters by source, outcome, and tool.

Your agent can now pause to ask you a question mid-task: a modal sheet pops over whatever tab you're on with the question (and optional context), you type or decline, and the agent resumes with your answer — important for safe peer-agent flows where an inbound Nostr request needs your consent before anything sensitive runs.
Removed the legacy `delegate` agent tool — the TENEX async-delegation surface has been replaced by lighter-weight peer-conversation primitives.

Your agent can now end a Nostr conversation cleanly with `end_conversation` and send a kind:1 note to a friend with `send_friend_message` — both are peer-only and surface inside the active conversation thread.

Your agent's Nostr inbox now speaks NIP-42: when a relay challenges the connection with AUTH, the app signs a kind:22242 event with your key and re-issues the subscription once accepted — so auth-gated relays will actually deliver events to you instead of silently dropping them.

Settings > Agent now has a Conversations screen listing every Nostr thread your agent has participated in, with names + avatars pulled from kind:0 metadata and a one-tap JSONL export of the raw events.

When a Nostr message lands from a pubkey you haven't approved, a trust-this-user sheet now pops over whatever tab you're on with the message preview and one-tap Allow / Block — no more digging into Settings to react.

Wiki page compilation is now an opt-in agent skill — the agent loads focused instructions on citation grounding, scopes, and page kinds before building or removing a page. Read queries still work without it.

Fixed: when you ask the agent to generate an episode with clips from a podcast you haven't downloaded yet, it now downloads the audio automatically before stitching — no more silent fallback to narration.

The assistant now has opt-in skills. The first is podcast generation — ask it to make a TLDR or mock interview and it loads instructions, lists your ElevenLabs voices, and stitches a custom episode into your 'Agent Generated' podcast with auto-chapters.
The assistant now publishes its Nostr profile (name, bio, picture) so contacts can discover it by public key.

The assistant can now build, list, and delete wiki pages on demand — just ask it to 'compile a wiki page on Zone 2' or 'build a page about Peter Attia' and it searches your transcripts, verifies every claim, and saves it so the system keeps it fresh as new episodes arrive.
Home now always shows your podcasts as a list — the grid option has been removed.
The Wiki tab now uses the same background and visual style as the rest of the app.
What's New now remembers what you've already read even if you swipe the sheet away instead of tapping Got it.
Closing the agent chat no longer stops the agent mid-task — it keeps working in the background. A red dot on the sparkles button appears when the agent has replied while the sheet was closed.
Episodes the agent plays from podcasts you haven't subscribed to now show up in Current Listens, open correctly in Episode Detail, and get a transcript and chapters just like any other episode.

Agent-generated podcasts now auto-build a transcript and chapters from their script — no extra steps. When a clip from another episode plays, the artwork swaps to that show's cover and a chip appears so you can jump straight to the source episode.
Your profile now shows your real Nostr display name and picture after signing in with your nsec — no more auto-generated placeholders.

The agent now starts on a fast, cheap model and self-escalates to a stronger one when a task needs serious reasoning. Pick both the Initial and Thinking models in Settings → Models. Once the agent upgrades, the conversation stays on the stronger model until you start a new chat.

The assistant can now play any podcast episode without a subscription — just ask it to find and play a guest appearance or one-off episode. New tools: search the global podcast directory, subscribe to any show by feed URL, and play external episodes directly.
Agent chat toolbar now shows a + button to start a new conversation instead of a trash/delete button.
Delegate tool events now include the project 'a' tag so they're properly routed in TENEX.

Agent chats now have history — tap the clock in the top-left to revisit any past conversation, swipe to delete, or start a fresh thread. The agent auto-resumes recent chats and starts a new one after you've been away a few hours; titles for each thread are generated automatically.

Wiki citation chips now peek the cited moment on tap — long-press to commit and play the full clip. Failed compiles show a Retry button, and the auto-refresh toggle finally does something: enable it and your wiki pages update as new transcripts land.

Ask the agent to generate a custom audio episode — it can narrate a TLDR in any voice, weave in clips from your podcasts, or produce a multi-speaker fake interview — and the episode appears in your library ready to play.
The agent can now download and transcribe an episode in one step — ask it to transcribe something and it will wait until the transcript is ready before answering.
Episode pages now have a comments section powered by Nostr — replies appear under every episode and are shared across podcast apps that support Podcasting 2.0.
Tap targets fixed on all glass buttons. Speed now lives in the top-bar trailing cluster. Voice note mic anchors the left side of the transport row.
Ask the agent to play specific sections of an episode — it queues them in order with a subtle sound cue between each, so you can hear just the parts you care about.

The scrubber now shows your clips as highlights on the timeline — tap one to jump straight to it. Chapter breaks appear as faint tick marks. A new '< Jump back' button lets you return to where you were before any seek, like browser back.
AI-compiled chapters and ad-skip markers now survive feed refreshes — no more "Compiling chapters" flicker every time you reopen a podcast.

Tap the mic button in the player to record a voice note — the agent gets the timestamp, chapter, and what you said, then decides what to do with it (take a note, clip the moment, find a reference, whatever you need).
The in-app assistant can now save clips for you — just ask it to clip a moment and it'll appear in your Clippings tab.
New Clippings tab: every clip you've ever made, beautifully laid out with one tap to play from the exact moment.
Tap an episode to open its detail page; use the play button on the right to start playback directly from the list.
AssemblyAI is now available for transcription. Connect it with BYOK or a manual key, then choose it under Settings > Intelligence > Models > Speech.

Smarter clips and quotes: tap Snip or Share Quote and the LLM picks clean sentence boundaries from the surrounding transcript — no more mid-sentence cuts or missing the punchline you reacted to. Needs an AI key under Settings → AI keys.

New: Diagnostics sheet on every episode (••• → Diagnostics) shows the full audit log — what was downloaded, when, whether transcription was attempted, which provider, why it was skipped, and the exact error if it failed. Retry buttons let you re-run the pipeline and watch fresh events stream in.

AirPods gestures: double-tap and triple-tap now do what you want — skip, jump chapters, or clip the moment you just heard. Configure under Settings → Playback. Heads-up: iOS also surfaces these as next/previous-track buttons on the lock screen, Control Center, and CarPlay — those fire the same actions.
Player redesigned — compact artwork and episode info at the top, AirPlay and share in the toolbar, sleep timer in the ••• menu.
Search is now snappier — results only update after you pause typing, so fast keystrokes no longer cause lag.
What's New now stays out of the way — once you've seen an entry, you won't see it again, even after reinstalling. Fresh installs start silent; only entries shipped from now on will pop the sheet.
Fixed a bug where tapping any text field would immediately dismiss the keyboard — nothing was typeable.
Categorization now has its own model setting under AI → Models, so you can use a cheap model for one-shot category sorting without affecting the live agent chat.
Fixed a hard crash when Apple's on-device transcription kicked off — the analyzer was being driven twice through different init paths.
Transcripts now run automatically the moment an episode finishes downloading — no manual step. Apple's on-device transcription actually fires now (it used to fail before the file was on disk).
Apple on-device transcription is now available — uses iOS 26 SpeechTranscriber on Apple Silicon with no API key required. Switch to it under Settings → Intelligence → Models → Speech.
Transcription now supports OpenRouter Whisper in addition to ElevenLabs Scribe — switch providers under Settings → Intelligence → Models → Speech.
Mini-player now shows a progress bar at the bottom edge, Apple Music-style.
What's New now reliably shows the changelog on first launch — the sheet was rendering empty after onboarding because of a SwiftUI render race.
BYOK setup is now one flow: approve OpenRouter, ElevenLabs, Ollama Cloud, and Perplexity together during onboarding or from Settings.
Show pages got a redesign — artwork sits on the left next to the title and description, the tint gradient bleeds to the top of the screen, and episodes now show their own artwork in the feed.
Home screen is cleaner — removed unplayed count badges on artwork, the date header, and filter chips.
Perplexity online search now works with just your OpenRouter key — no separate Perplexity account required.
What's New now actually shows you what's new — the modal was always marking everything as seen before you could read it.
Removed the filter and layout menu from the home toolbar for a cleaner look.
The full-screen player now shows clearly readable elapsed and remaining times (e.g. 12:34 / -47:26) under the waveform scrubber.
Fixed the Now Playing screen clipping controls and text on both sides of the screen.

Episode rows now use a slim 2 px progress bar instead of text pills: primary-colored while downloading, accent-colored while transcribing. Not-yet-downloaded episodes are subtly dimmed so you can tell at a glance which ones are ready offline.

Tap the Home title to browse categories — each category shows podcast artwork, a description, and your unplayed count. Tap the gear on any category to set its transcription and wiki preferences independently. Selecting a category scopes the whole feed to that section.
Notification settings refresh when you come back from iOS Settings — flipping permission no longer leaves the banner stuck on 'Denied' until you reload the screen

Ad reads detected from the transcript now show an amber stripe and a speaker.slash glyph on the chapter list. Settings → Playback adds an Auto-skip ads toggle (off by default while detection quality settles). Pre-roll ads get a one-tap Skip button above the scrubber.

Episodes without publisher chapters now get AI-compiled chapters as soon as the transcript is ready — look for the small AI pill next to those titles. The player triggers compilation on first open too, so older episodes light up without re-ingesting.

Episode detail and the player now lead with chapters — the transcript reader and follow-along modes are gone. Transcripts still drive search, clips, and the agent in the background; you read chapters, not walls of text. Long-press a chapter to ask the agent about it.
Model selector search no longer rebuilds 200 lower-cased haystacks per keystroke — typing into the OpenRouter / Ollama list is snappier
Feedback search now matches across Unicode case folds (same fix the wiki just got)
Wiki search now finds pages across Unicode case folds — searching 'strasse' matches 'Straße'
Categories now reshape the whole Home like flipping to a different magazine section — agent picks, Resume rail, Threaded Today, and the dateline all re-curate to whichever category you're in
The agent's pick rationale changes voice per category — Learning is more 'continue this thread', Entertainment is more 'long-form, narrative', and so on
Track what's new — every install now shows a sheet of changes since you last opened the app
ElevenLabs Scribe transcripts now actually work — fixed the bogus polling endpoint and bumped the timeout to ten minutes
Big audio files no longer pre-upload from the device; ElevenLabs fetches them via source_url instead
Home now reads agent picks aloud and explains why each one was chosen
Threaded Today pill and topic-pivot lenses let you jump between angles on a story without leaving Home
Audio no longer cuts out when the screen locks during playback
Player action chips collapsed to icon-only Liquid Glass buttons so the controls breathe
Segmented pickers everywhere now use the new Liquid Glass control with proper haptics
Removed serif fonts across the app — everything now uses one consistent rounded type ramp
Spotlight previews of episodes now show clean text snippets instead of raw HTML
Long-press any transcript line to ask the agent about that exact moment
Settings now has a Downloads Manager so you can see and free up space from cached episodes
Player accessibility, skip glyphs, and scrubber polished — the chrome finally feels finished
Today and Library merged into one editorial Home — dateline, filter chips, featured row, and your shows all in one surface
Episodes without a publisher transcript now auto-transcribe in the background via ElevenLabs Scribe
More playback-speed presets so you can dial in the exact pace you like
Fixed iTunes Search returning shows that wouldn't subscribe — the results now match by parsed feed URL
